strengths: Hands-on ETL and data pipeline experience
Experience with BI tools (Power BI, Tableau)
weaknesses: Limited explicit consumer credit or underwriting experience
No direct portfolio risk monitoring examples
No fraud detection or merchant risk experience noted
Only 2+ years in relevant analyst roles
improvement_bullets: Developed portfolio analytics and monitoring frameworks by automating data validation routines, ensuring early detection of out-of-trend performance and data drifts.
Partnered with engineering and product teams to integrate machine learning model outputs into credit risk monitoring dashboards, facilitating real-time assessment of underwriting efficacy.
Executed attribution analysis on marketing campaigns to identify key drivers of loan performance and assess the risk-adjusted profitability of various merchant segments.
Applied statistical modeling techniques to evaluate macroeconomic trends, translating external economic shifts into actionable adjustments for credit risk policies.
Collaborated on fraud detection initiatives by analyzing transaction-level data to identify anomalous patterns and refine rules-based merchant risk mitigation.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Analyst II, Credit Risk Analytics opportunity with Affirm. I am interested in this opportunity because of Affirm’s mission to make credit more honest and transparent through data-driven innovation. What interests me about working for Affirm is its approach to reinventing consumer finance, which intrigues me because of the company’s focus on using data to balance sustainable growth with consumer-friendly products. I believe my technical proficiency in SQL and Python and my experience in building analytical frameworks would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by translating raw data into clear, strategic business insights. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified high-value customer segments through statistical modeling and targeted marketing initiatives. By leveraging these models, I delivered a 300% increase in revenue within the targeted segment over 12 months, a result that directly speaks to my ability to drive profitability through data-backed decisions. Ultimately, I know that the Analyst II, Credit Risk Analytics role at Affirm would allow me to bring my expertise in data automation and portfolio monitoring to managing risk in a dynamic, high-growth environment. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst at Shine Masters enabled me to develop my cross-functional partnership skills. I regularly translated complex technical findings into business recommendations for diverse stakeholders, ensuring alignment between data science and operational goals. This experience is directly transferable to collaborating with your Machine Learning and Quantitative Market teams to refine underwriting models. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in Python-based ETL and data pipeline architecture in working on portfolio risk management at Affirm. I know that my analytical rigor and communication skills align with what it takes to succeed at the Analyst II, Credit Risk Analytics role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
